assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of this painting?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/757a4e6a48754a23a642efba9190209f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The wife was not pregnant and may have been deceased at the time the painting was created.**

choices.content: Giovanni Arnolfini, an Italian merchant who worked in Bruges, was the patron of this painting.
Giovanni Arnolfini's wealth is an important theme of the painting, with evidence of his wealth in many details.
The gestures and details are symbols that have meaning in this painting, but it is difficult to determine the particular meaning or purpose of this painting.
Giovanni Arnolfini's wife was pregnant with their first child at the time of this painting.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The removed shoes may symbolize that . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a939319125fcc81f125852d78faadfa4.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The removed shoes may symbolize that the couple stands on holy ground.**

choices.content: the couple stands on holy ground.
it was customary at that time to remove shoes while indoors.
the woman's devotion to her husband.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What allowed Jan van Eyck to create the luminous quality of the painting?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bc968f2ffe19b5787456c27e4bceb68f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Jan van Eyck used multiple layers of thinned-out oil paint, known as glazes, to create the luminous quality of the painting.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**This painting is typical of other works from the Northern Renaissance because of its . . .**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/757a4e6a48754a23a642efba9190209f.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **This painting shows the same attention to detail that is present in other works of art from the Northern Renaissance.**
